\newpage

\hypertarget{List of Acronyms}{\chapter*{List of Acronyms}\label{acronyms}}
\addcontentsline{toc}{chapter}{List of Acronyms}


TODO: add list of acronyms using glossaries package

```latex
\usepackage{glossaries}

define acronyms somewhere, e.g.:
\newacronym{ar}{AR}{Augmented Reality}
\newacronym{hmdmr}{HMDMR}{Head-Mounted Mixed Reality}

use them in text, e.g.:
The study focuses on \gls{ar} and \gls{hmdmr}.

first use will show full name and acronym. thereafter, always the acronym, with all instances hyperlinked back to the list

generate the list here:
\printglossaries
```
